vitals.location: Uçhisar, Cappadocia, Turkey (historical course; current Büyük Sürüş ride is Ürgüp-based)
vitals.date_specific: Discontinued as a competitive gran fondo; the Cappadocia Bike Festival's current road component (Büyük Sürüş) is an open-traffic, noncompetitive ride, most recently 18 July 2026

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57.

course_description.character: IMPORTANT — this profile describes a competitive gran fondo that no longer exists. Argeus launched Turkey's first Gran Fondo inside the Cappadocia Bike Festival in 2015, with 90km/2,000m+ and 47km/600m+ (and later a 108km long/41km short Uçhisar-based) courses through the fairy-chimney landscape. That competitive gran fondo format ended after 2018 (the research does not confirm whether roads were fully closed to traffic). The festival's current road offering is the Büyük Sürüş — an open-traffic, noncompetitive group ride (most recently 112km/1,700m+, 18 July 2026, Ürgüp-based) that explicitly states in its own terms that it is 'not a race.'
course_description.signature_challenge: Historically: the fairy-chimney descent into Ürgüp on the old 41km short course. That course no longer runs as a race.
suffering_zones: Historical short course (pre-2019): a fast descent past the iconic fairy chimneys into Ürgüp, then a climb toward Mustafapaşa through Ayvalı and Bahçeli back to Uçhisar — this routing is retired, not currently run as a competitive event.

history.origin_story: Launched by Argeus (a Cappadocia tour operator founded 1993 by Aydın Ayhan Güney, Muammer Sak and Veli Tören) as Turkey's first Gran Fondo, staged inside the Cappadocia Bike Festival.
notable_moments: A rider died during the 2018 short-course race after missing a bend and striking a tree and road sign — contemporary rider discussion (participant testimony, not a formal investigation) criticized late publication of the risk report and called for the km-11 section to be removed.
history.reputation: Discontinued as a competitive race after 2018; the festival itself survives as a noncompetitive open-traffic ride (Büyük Sürüş), a real but materially different product than the gran fondo this profile was built around.

biased_opinion.verdict: Defunct — Do Not List As A Race
biased_opinion.summary: The Cappadocia Gran Fondo you'd find described online — competitive, fairy-chimney descents — stopped happening after 2018, the same year a rider died on the short course after striking a tree off a missed bend. The Cappadocia Bike Festival itself is still alive, but its current road component, Büyük Sürüş, is an open-traffic, noncompetitive group ride that the organizer's own terms describe as 'not a race.' If you're picking this profile because you want a scored, competitive gran fondo through Cappadocia's volcanic landscape, that event doesn't exist anymore.
strengths: The historical course concept was genuinely distinctive — competitive racing through fairy-chimney terrain
The festival brand survived, so the region hasn't lost cycling tourism infrastructure entirely
The current Büyük Sürüş ride still lets riders see the fairy-chimney landscape on a bike, just without race stakes or closed roads
weaknesses: The competitive gran fondo format this profile describes ended after 2018 and has not been revived
A rider fatality in the final competitive edition (2018) is a serious, documented safety incident on the discontinued course
The current replacement (Büyük Sürüş) is explicitly non-competitive and shares open traffic with vehicles — a different risk and experience profile than a closed-road fondo
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Don't book this expecting a race. If you want to ride Cappadocia's fairy-chimney roads on an organized, non-competitive group ride, the festival's Büyük Sürüş is real and current — but it is not the closed-course gran fondo this database entry was originally built to describe.

final_verdict.one_liner: A once-real Cappadocia gran fondo, last raced in 2018 — the year a rider died on the short course — with no revival since; the festival lives on as a noncompetitive group ride, not a race.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Skip it as a 'race' — it isn't one anymore. If you want to ride the Cappadocia landscape on an organized group ride, look at the festival's current Büyük Sürüş instead, with clear eyes that it's open-traffic and noncompetitive.
final_verdict.alternatives: For a real competitive road gran fondo elsewhere in Turkey, check the Turkish Cycling Federation's current 2026 gran fondo calendar (bisiklet.org.tr) rather than this discontinued event.

catalog_flags.status_note: Marked defunct this wave: the competitive, closed-course Cappadocia Gran Fondo ended after 2018 (a rider fatality that year). The Cappadocia Bike Festival's current road offering, Büyük Sürüş, is a separate, noncompetitive, open-traffic ride whose own terms state it is 'not a race' — flagging for a human call on whether to keep this as a historical/defunct entry or tombstone it outright.

rider_intel.search_text: Purged this wave: 4 of 5 videos were generic Cappadocia tourism/cycling-trip footage or a different event (Uluslararası Kapadokya Bisiklet Turu, an unrelated stage race) with no rider commentary on the Gran Fondo itself. Retained only the one video that directly documents the historical Gran Fondo's short-course start (2015).

eligibility.notes: The competitive, closed-course Cappadocia Gran Fondo ended after 2018 (a rider fatality that year); no revival since. The Cappadocia Bike Festival itself is alive and its current road offering, Büyük Sürüş (curl-verified 200, most recently 18 July 2026), is a separate, noncompetitive, open-traffic ride whose own terms state it is 'not a race.'
